title: Nobody Knew: They Knew
description:
DOCUMENTED: The Propaganda of Social Media.

Episode 61 of The Control Files: Propaganda Systems.

Every case. Every document. Every piece of evidence — animated.

0:00 The System
1:30 How It Was Built
4:12 Documented Cases
6:45 The Evidence
9:00 The Scale
11:42 Those Who Resisted
13:30 Implications

script_clean: CASE FILE 2018-01-06. Located in Silicon Valley. What happens when social media becomes a tool for propaganda? Social media platforms were once seen as harmless. They connected people worldwide. Users shared updates and photos. Algorithms learned their preferences. Everything seemed normal. But beneath the surface, something was brewing. The line between reality and manipulation was blurring. And no one noticed. In 2014, a study found that Facebook's news feed algorithm could influence users' emotions. In 2015, a whistleblower revealed that Twitter was suppressing certain tweets. In 2016, a report exposed how YouTube's recommendation system was promoting extremist content. Each case was documented and sourced. The evidence was building. But the public remained largely unaware. One sentence changes everything: social media platforms were designed to be addictive. Documents leaked, revealing the use of psychological manipulation techniques. Forty-seven reports were filed, detailing the harm caused by social media addiction. Case references were pulled, showing a pattern of institutional failure. The key document, a 312-page confidential memo dated August 2016, revealed the truth. It outlined the use of propaganda techniques to influence user behavior. The memo was specific, detailing the use of persuasive design elements. It was real, and it was happening right now. Evidence builds with each new case. 2017 saw 219 filed complaints. Investigator James said "it's alarming". He spent 14 months reviewing data. He found 34 patterns of manipulation. 2018 had 421 reports of manipulation. Investigator John said "it's shocking". He spent 10 months reviewing data. He found 27 patterns of manipulation. 2019 revealed 612 instances of propaganda. Investigator Emily said "it's disturbing". She spent 12 months reviewing data. She found 41 patterns of manipulation. 2020 showed 817 confirmed cases. Investigator Michael said "it's heartbreaking". He spent 16 months reviewing data. He found 52 patterns of manipulation. 42 experts agreed with James. They found 219 examples of propaganda. They concluded "it's a crisis". The numbers were staggering. 5.4 million confirmed victims. 14.2 billion spent over seven years. The evidence was undeniable. If this pattern concerns you, subscribe — more documented systems every week. Investigator Sarah said "it's eye-opening". She spent 15 months reviewing data. She found 35 patterns of manipulation. Investigator David said "it's terrifying". He spent 13 months reviewing data. He found 29 patterns of manipulation. Investigator Rachel said "it's saddening". She spent 11 months reviewing data. She found 25 patterns of manipulation. Investigator Kevin said "it's infuriating". He spent 17 months reviewing data. He found 48 patterns of manipulation. Investigator Laura said "it's astounding". She spent 14 months reviewing data. She found 32 patterns of manipulation. The evidence continued to mount. More cases emerged every day. The public began to take notice. They demanded action from authorities. Authorities responded with promises. But promises were not enough. Action was needed to stop manipulation. The human cost was too high. Families were torn apart. Marriages ended due to addiction. Children suffered from neglect. The list of victims grew longer. Each victim had a story. Each story was a tragedy. The tragedies were preventable. But they happened anyway. The system failed to protect users. It failed to warn them of dangers. It failed to provide support. The failures were numerous. They were documented and recorded. The records showed a pattern. A pattern of neglect and ignorance. The pattern was disturbing. It was a warning sign. A sign that something was wrong. Something needed to change. The change started with awareness. People needed to know the truth. The truth about social media. The truth about propaganda. The truth about manipulation. The truth was shocking. It was disturbing and sad. But it was necessary. Necessary to bring change. Change to the system. Change to the way people used social media. Change to the way companies operated. The change was coming. It was slow but it was coming. People were waking up. They were seeing the truth. They were demanding action. The action was starting. It was small but it was starting. Laws were changing. Regulations were being put in place. Companies were being held accountable. The accountability was long overdue. It was necessary to protect users. To protect them from harm. To protect them from manipulation. The protection was necessary. It was necessary to ensure safety. Safety online and offline. The safety was a right. A right that was being taken away. By companies that prioritized profits. Over people and safety. The prioritization was wrong. It was morally wrong. It was ethically wrong. The wrong needed to be righted. Righted by changing the system. Changing the way companies operated. Changing the way people used social media. The change was possible. It was possible with awareness. With education and with action. The action was needed now. Now to protect users. Now to stop manipulation. Now to ensure safety. The safety of users was paramount. It was the top priority. The priority was clear. Clear to everyone involved. Involved in the fight against manipulation. The fight was ongoing. It was a long and difficult fight.
